methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axial Composites is a Seed-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Giuseppe Rapisarda, having raised $1M+. The broader sector comprises 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though tight peer clustering at the Seed stage limits direct comparable valuation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $1M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $2.5M. A peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow-stage peers within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector—while 11 same-sector companies exist, fewer than 3 met adjacent-stage or similar-tier filters needed for statistical validity. Peer-driven triangulation would have introduced unwarranted precision.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.1M (low) to $2.9M (high), with a median of $2.5M. This single-method triangulation carries execution risk as the primary determinant; valuation confidence would strengthen with evidence of product traction, customer commitments, or revised fundraising trajectory. The $1M raised proxy itself warrants confirmation, as reported figures may include notes or contingent commitments that affect effective dilution.
